nativeRange.habitat: Lagoon and near-shore reefs, typically associated with branching corals at depths of roughly 1-38 m.
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical coral reef among branching Acropora and similar corals, often in small aggregations.

diet.notes: In the wild, feeds primarily on zooplankton above the substrate. In captivity, generally accepts a varied diet of quality marine flakes, small pellets, frozen mysis and brine shrimp, and chopped meaty seafoods; occasional dried marine algae rounds out the diet.

avoidWith: newly added tankmates after the damsel is established

summary: The Azure Damselfish (Chrysiptera hemicyanea) is a small Indo-Pacific reef fish with a vivid blue front half and a yellow rear. Generally considered one of the hardier marine fish for beginners, it tends to become territorial as it matures and is best kept as a single specimen in modest reef aquariums.
careNotes: Generally considered one of the hardiest and most beginner-friendly marine fish — tolerates a wide range of conditions and accepts prepared foods readily. Most commonly kept as a single specimen because it tends to be aggressive toward conspecifics and toward newly introduced tankmates after it has established a territory; multiples sometimes work in larger reef tanks (roughly 200 L and up) with abundant rockwork that breaks lines of sight. Provide plenty of live rock with caves and branching coral for shelter, plus some open swimming space in the mid-to-lower water column. ORA and other facilities offer captive-bred specimens, which tend to be better adapted to aquarium life than wild-caught fish.
breedingNotes: Has been bred in captivity (notably by ORA). Forms distinct pairs and is oviparous; demersal eggs are deposited on a rocky substrate and guarded/aerated by the male until hatching. Without a pelagic larval phase to mimic, larval rearing is still demanding for hobbyists.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Care level, temperament, reef compatibility, water parameters (pH 8.1-8.4, dKH 8-12, SG 1.020-1.025, 72-78 F), 30 US gal minimum, max ~2.75 in.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed taxonomy (Pomacentridae), max length 7.0 cm TL, depth 1-38 m, Indo-West Pacific distribution, water temperature 22-28 C, IUCN Vulnerable status.

additional.name: AlgaeBarn (Keeping the Azure Damselfish)
additional.notes: Cross-checked size (3 in / ~8 cm), confirmed reef-safe and high conspecific aggression, confirmed ORA captive-bred availability, lifespan 2-6 yr wild and up to ~15 yr in captivity, breeding behavior (pair-bonding, demersal eggs, male egg-guarding).

fish.breedingNotes: Captive-bred by commercial facilities (ORA) but larval rearing remains challenging for hobbyists; pairs spawn readily on rockwork and the male guards the eggs.
